module_map.responsibility: Tokenizer base and token type definitions; converts SQL string into Token stream. Delegates core tokenization to compiled tokenizer_core module.
AST parser; consumes Token stream from tokenizer and produces expression tree. Dialect-specific parsers inherit from this. Core parsing logic, precedence rules, and expression building.
Expression type definitions (AST nodes). Organized by category: string.py, functions.py, math.py, dml.py, ddl.py, query.py, etc. Defines schema and arg_types for all SQL constructs.
SQL code generation from AST. Converts expression tree back to SQL string. Dialect-specific generators override generation logic. ~243KB, largest module.
Dialect-specific parsers and generators. Each dialect (bigquery.py, snowflake.py, postgres.py, etc.) inherits from base Dialect, overrides tokenizer, parser, and generator behavior.
Dialect-specific parser extensions. Each dialect parser (parsers/bigquery.py, parsers/snowflake.py, etc.) extends Parser with dialect-specific syntax rules.
Query optimization passes. Rewrites AST for performance (simplification, pushdown, elimination). Used after parsing, before generation.
Query execution runtime. Evaluates parsed/optimized AST. Includes table context, Python backend, and environment setup.
Utility functions: seq_get, csv, name_sequence, ensure_list. Shared across modules.

types_produced: TokenType enum (SELECT, FROM, WHERE, IDENTIFIER, etc.)
Select, From, Where, Join, FunctionCall nodes

pipeline_trace.module: expressions/ (core.py, string.py, query.py, dml.py, ddl.py, etc.)
pipeline_trace.key_function: Expression.__init__(...) - node construction with typed arg_types
types_produced: Literal, Identifier, Column, Table, Join, etc.
Transformed Expression tree (same types, different structure)

pipeline_trace.key_function: sqlglot.parse(sql: str, read: str, write: str) -> str

cross_module_hubs.reason: Hub for all expression types. Parser, generator, optimizer, and all dialect modules import and use Expression/Func. Defines base node types and arg_types schema.
Central parser logic consumed by all dialect parsers (parsers/ subdirectory). Imports tokens, expressions, errors. Heavily used in parse pipeline.
Central generator consumed by all dialect generators (dialects/). Produces SQL output from AST. Large file with expression-to-SQL mapping for all types.

new_types: Already exists: Levenshtein class in expressions/string.py (lines 74-82) with arg_types: this, expression, ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost, max_dist
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: LEVENSHTEIN already implemented. Follow Soundex pattern (lines 144-149 in string.py): simple Expression + Func subclass with no custom arg_types (default single argument). For LEVENSHTEIN with distance parameters, it follows the pattern of functions with optional cost arguments (ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost, max_dist).
change_proposal.integration_point:
1. Expression class: Already in sqlglot/expressions/string.py lines 74-82
2. Parser support: Add keyword mapping in parser.py FUNCTIONS dict (similar to SOUNDEX)
3. Generator support: Add levenshtein_sql or levenshtein_<dialect>_sql method in generator.py (follow Soundex generation patterns)
4. Dialect integration: Override in dialects/snowflake.py, dialects/postgres.py, etc. if dialect-specific syntax needed (e.g., SnowflakeDialect.Generator overrides soundex_sql)
5. Tokenizer: Keyword 'LEVENSHTEIN' added via dialect KEYWORDS dict (e.g., ClickHouse, PostgreSQL define it)
risks: LEVENSHTEIN already defined but may not be fully integrated into all dialects. Check: (1) Is it in parser.py FUNCTIONS dict? (2) Do all major dialects have generator methods? (3) Dialect-specific cost parameters (PostgreSQL vs T-SQL vs MySQL variants) - parameter names/order differ.
Snowflake may use EDITDISTANCE instead of LEVENSHTEIN - need alias support via _sql_names field
Some dialects may not support distance parameters (ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost, max_dist) - need unsupported_args decorator on generator methods
Missing executor backend: Even if parsed/generated correctly, executor.py may not evaluate LEVENSHTEIN without Python implementation in executor/python.py
Test coverage: Verify existing tests in tests/dialects/test_*.py cover LEVENSHTEIN parsing, generation, and execution

tool_usage.params: find /tmp/sqlglot -type f -name '*.py' | head -20 - list directory structure
/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/parser.py limit 50 - examine parser entry point and imports
/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/tokens.py limit 80 - examine tokenizer and token types
SOUNDEX|EDITDISTANCE - find similar functions for pattern matching
class Soundex|class EditDistance - locate expression definitions
/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/expressions/string.py limit 150 - examine Levenshtein and Soundex classes
